title: ranunculus flammula var. alismifolius
intro: Ranunculus flammula var. alismifolius, also known as lesser spearwort, is a perennial aquatic plant belonging to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of alkaloids and other compounds. The health benefits of Ranunculus flammula var. alismifolius include its potential to reduce inflammation, fight infections, and exhibit antioxidant activities. Traditionally, the plant has been prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil for its medicinal uses, although its application and efficacy may require further scientific investigation.
